title: BookMyShow Seat Finder
description: BookMyShow stores seat numbers of a sold-out PVR show in ascending order. Given the sorted array `seats` and a query `target`, return the index of the seat if found, else -1. Must run in O(log n).
hints: sorted hai toh classic binary search soch
lo=0, hi=n-1; mid = lo + (hi-lo)/2
match mile toh return mid, warna lo/hi adjust kar

title: Razorpay Fee Floor Index
description: Razorpay's fee tiers are stored sorted ascending in `fees`. Given a transaction amount `amt`, return the index of the smallest fee that is >= `amt` (lower bound). If no such fee exists return `fees.length`.
hints: lower_bound classic
agar fees[mid] >= amt toh hi = mid (mid bhi candidate hai)
loop condition lo < hi rakh, return lo

title: Zomato Cuisine Price Ceiling
description: Zomato sorts dish prices ascending in `prices`. Given a budget `b`, return the index of the smallest price strictly greater than `b` (upper bound). If none, return `prices.length`.
hints: upper_bound: pehla index jahan value > b ho
fees[mid] > b -> hi = mid, warna lo = mid+1
half-open hi = n use kar

title: Flipkart Stock Count by SKU
description: Flipkart's warehouse log stores SKU IDs in non-decreasing order; duplicates indicate multiple units of the same SKU. Given `log` and a SKU `q`, return how many units exist (count of `q` in the array) using O(log n).
hints: upper_bound(q) - lower_bound(q) = count
do binary searches: first occurrence aur last+1
edge: empty array return 0

title: Swiggy Delivery Radius Sqrt
description: Swiggy needs the integer floor of square root of `area` to size delivery zones (no Math.sqrt allowed). Given non-negative integer `area`, return floor(sqrt(area)) using binary search on answer space.
hints: answer space [0, area]
mid*mid compare karne se overflow ho sakta — use BigInt ya mid <= area/mid
largest mid jahan mid*mid <= area

title: Meesho Price Bucket First Match
description: Meesho's catalog `prices` is sorted with possible duplicates (same item priced same across resellers). Find the first index where `prices[i] === target`, else -1.
hints: lower_bound nikaalo, phir verify karo arr[lb] === target
agar lb === n ya arr[lb] !== target return -1
duplicates handle karna important

title: Atlassian Jira Last Status Index
description: Atlassian ranks tickets by status code stored sorted ascending in `codes`. Given `q`, return the LAST index where `codes[i] === q`. If absent, return -1.
hints: upper_bound - 1 candidate hai
verify karo codes[ub-1] === q
edge: ub === 0 means absent

title: Postman API Version Insert Position
description: Postman maintains sorted API version numbers in `versions`. Given a new version `v`, return the index where it should be inserted to keep order (leftmost valid). If `v` already exists, return its first index.
hints: yeh exactly lower_bound hai
answer 0 se n tak ho sakta
no need to handle 'not found' separately

title: PhonePe Rotated Balance Log Search
description: PhonePe's daily settlement log is a strictly increasing array that has been rotated once at an unknown pivot. All elements are unique. Given `log` and a `target`, return its index, else -1, in O(log n).
hints: mid pe ek half guaranteed sorted hota hai
check arr[lo] <= arr[mid] for left-sorted
target us sorted half mein hai? haan toh wahi search, warna doosra

title: Cred Minimum Reward Pivot
description: Cred's reward array was originally sorted ascending then rotated once. All elements unique. Return the minimum value in O(log n).
hints: compare arr[mid] with arr[hi]
agar arr[mid] > arr[hi] toh min right half mein
warna mid candidate hai, hi = mid

title: Uber Surge Peak Hour
description: Uber's hourly demand `demand[i]` differs from neighbors (`demand[i] !== demand[i+1]`). A peak is any index `i` where `demand[i]` is strictly greater than both neighbors (treat out-of-bounds as -Infinity). Return the index of ANY peak in O(log n).
hints: climb up: agar arr[mid] < arr[mid+1] toh peak right mein hai
warna left half mein
hi = mid (mid khud peak ho sakta), lo = mid+1

title: Amazon Warehouse Grid Lookup
description: Amazon's warehouse grid `grid` (m x n) has each row sorted ascending and the first element of each row greater than the last of previous row (so flattening yields a sorted array). Given `target`, return true if present.
hints: treat as 1D sorted array of length m*n
index k -> (k/n, k%n) mapping
ek hi binary search, O(log(m*n))

title: Google Koko-style Server Throughput
description: Google has `piles` of pending log batches and `H` hours to finish. Each hour the server processes up to `k` items from one pile (if pile has fewer, it finishes that pile and moves on next hour). Return the minimum integer `k` such that all piles are processed within `H` hours.
hints: binary search on answer space [1, max(piles)]
feasible(k) = sum(ceil(p/k)) <= H
smallest k jahan feasible true ho

title: Swiggy Delivery Capacity Allocator
description: Swiggy must dispatch packages `weights` IN ORDER over `D` days using a single truck. Each day, the truck carries some prefix of remaining packages whose sum <= capacity. Return the minimum truck capacity to finish in `D` days.
hints: lo = max(weights), hi = sum(weights)
feasible(cap): greedy split, count days needed
smallest cap jahan days <= D

title: Zomato Rotated Restaurant Ratings
description: Zomato's ratings array (with possible duplicates) was sorted ascending and rotated once. Given `ratings` and `target`, return true if target exists.
hints: duplicates ki wajah se arr[lo] === arr[mid] === arr[hi] case mein lo++/hi--
warna usual rotated logic
worst case O(n) jab saare duplicates

title: Microsoft First Bad Deploy
description: Microsoft has `n` sequential deploys; once a deploy goes bad, all subsequent are bad. Given `n` and a boolean array `bad` (1-indexed conceptually but here 0-indexed) where `bad[i]` is true if deploy `i+1` is bad, return the 1-indexed number of the first bad deploy. Guaranteed at least one bad.
hints: monotonic predicate isBad(i)
find first index where isBad true
return index+1 (1-indexed)

title: Paytm Transaction Mountain Summit
description: Paytm's hourly transaction array `tx` strictly increases up to a peak then strictly decreases (mountain). Length >= 3. Return the index of the peak in O(log n).
hints: compare tx[mid] with tx[mid+1]
ascending ho toh peak right (lo = mid+1)
descending ho toh peak at mid or left (hi = mid)

title: Flipkart Nth Root Bucket
description: Flipkart sizes warehouse buckets by `floor(x^(1/n))`. Given non-negative integer `x` and integer `n` (1 <= n <= 30), return floor of the n-th root of x using binary search.
hints: answer space [0, x]; for n>=2 narrow to [0, x] still works
compute mid^n iteratively, break early if exceeds x
watch overflow with BigInt or early break

title: Amazon Fixed Point Magic Index
description: Amazon's distinct sorted array `arr` may contain a magic index where `arr[i] === i`. Return the smallest such index, else -1, in O(log n).
hints: arr distinct sorted -> arr[i]-i is non-decreasing
find smallest i where arr[i]-i >= 0 AND arr[i]-i === 0
binary search for arr[i] === i

title: Google EV Charging Station Spread
description: Google maps has `n` candidate parking spots at positions `pos` (sorted ascending). You must place `k` EV chargers at distinct spots so that the minimum distance between any two chargers is maximized. Return that max min-distance.
hints: binary search on distance d in [1, max-min]
feasible(d): greedy place at first, then next pos >= last+d
agar count >= k toh feasible

title: BookMyShow Combined Show Median
description: BookMyShow merges sorted ticket-price arrays from two shows: `a` and `b`. Return the median of the combined sorted list in O(log(min(m,n))). If total length is even, return the average of the two middle values.
hints: partition both arrays so left half has (m+n+1)/2 elems
binary search on shorter array's partition
max(left_a, left_b) <= min(right_a, right_b) means valid partition

title: Amazon Allocate Shipping Bins
description: Amazon has `n` packages with weights `pkg` (in given order) to distribute to `k` workers. Each worker takes a CONTIGUOUS subarray; every package must go to exactly one worker; each worker gets >= 1 package. Minimize the maximum total weight assigned to any worker. Return -1 if k > n.
hints: binary search on max_load in [max(pkg), sum(pkg)]
feasible(L): greedy partition, count workers needed
agar workers <= k toh feasible

title: Google Paint Fence Partition
description: Google has fence boards with lengths `boards` to be painted by `painters`. Each painter paints a CONTIGUOUS sequence of boards at 1 unit time per length unit, all in parallel. Minimize total finish time = max painter load. Return -1 if painters > boards.length.
hints: classic binary search on time T
feasible(T): greedy assign, count painters
lo = max(boards), hi = sum

title: Razorpay Kth Smallest Settlement Pair
description: Razorpay has two sorted (ascending) settlement arrays `a` and `b`. For every pair (i, j), define sum = a[i] + b[j]. Return the k-th SMALLEST such pair sum (1-indexed).
hints: binary search on answer S
count pairs with sum <= S using two-pointer in O(m+n)
smallest S where count >= k

title: Uber Minimum Time to Complete Trips
description: Uber has drivers; driver `i` takes `time[i]` minutes per trip and works without breaks. Given `totalTrips` to be completed across the fleet, return the minimum total minutes needed (driver i completes floor(t / time[i]) trips by minute t).
hints: binary search on minutes t
feasible(t) = sum(floor(t / time[i])) >= totalTrips
lo=1, hi=min(time) * totalTrips
